images.caption: Figure 1:Comparison of self-supervision signals.R-Zero[10]uses majority voting, restricting it to verifiable closed-domain tasks. LLM-as-a-Judge assigns scalar scores, bounded by the judge’s capability. In contrast, G-Zero creates an internal preference signal by preferring hint-conditioned responses over unassisted ones, eliminating the need for external verifiers or judges.

Figure 2:The G-Zero co-evolutionary loop.Top (Proposer training):The ProposerπP\pi_{P}generates query–hint pairs{(qi,hi)}\{(q_{i},h_{i})\}. The frozen GeneratorπG\pi_{G}produces unassisted responses, and Hint-δ\deltais computed from the log-probability shift each hint induces on the Generator’s distribution. Theδ\deltavalues serve as the GRPO reward, drivingπP\pi_{P}to explore the Generator’s blind spots.Bottom (Generator training):WithπP\pi_{P}frozen, the Generator answers each query with and without the hint; We then filter the resulting pairs into a preference dataset𝒟R+1\mathcal{D}_{R+1}, whereRRis the round index, on whichπG\pi_{G}is updated via DPO.

Figure 3:Performance change (Δ\Delta) relative to the base model across incremental DPO pool sizes (N∈{100,200,400,730}N\in\{100,200,400,730\}) versus the from-scratch optimization in Round 2 (star atN=730N=730).

Figure 3:Performance change (Δ\Delta) relative to the base model across incremental DPO pool sizes (N∈{100,200,400,730}N\in\{100,200,400,730\}) versus the from-scratch optimization in Round 2 (star atN=730N=730).

Figure 4:Empirical distributions of Hint-δ\deltafor Round 1 and Round 2. The dashed vertical lines denote the medianδ\deltafor each round. The rightward shift in Round 2 demonstrates the co-evolutionary dynamic: as the Generator becomes more capable, the Proposer adapts by synthesizing increasingly impactful hints, thereby pushing the intrinsic learning signal to a higher baseline.
